The output contains sections corresponding to all three pages of the source PDF: 'NC OneMap Update' (Slide 1), 'Data Releases' (Slide 2), and 'Tasks' (Slide 3).
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

checks.a_analysis: The content from each slide is represented exactly once in the JSON output. There are no instances of paragraphs, list items, or sections being repeated. The slide titles and bulleted items appear in the output as they do in the source without duplication.

The JSON organizes the document into three sections corresponding to the three slides. Each section has a 'title' field which serves as the heading for that slide's content. This correctly mirrors the hierarchy of a presentation where each slide begins with a major heading. No heading levels are skipped in the logical structure.
The source document contains no tables, and no tables were generated in the output.

The reading order in the JSON follows the sequence of the slides (1, 2, 3) and the top-to-bottom layout within each slide. For example, on slide 2, the 'Since last GICC meeting' text correctly precedes the 'Natural Heritage Program' list, and the website URL follows it, matching the visual intent.

Lists are accurately identified and nested. On page 2, the 'Natural Heritage Program' is correctly rendered as the parent of a nested list containing four sub-items. On page 3, the complex multi-level hierarchy (Ongoing > Infrastructure buildout > Replication tasks) is perfectly preserved through nested list objects in the JSON.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The HTML metadata reports 0 headings in the JSON but 3 in the rendered HTML. This mismatch of 3 headings is right at the threshold of the check. However, the JSON explicitly includes 3 'title' fields within its 'sections' array. The renderer correctly identified these section titles as headings to produce 3 semantic headings in the HTML. Since the structural intent is preserved and the content is present, this is not a defect.

The URL 'data.nconemap.com' found on slide 2 is correctly captured as text in the JSON. The HTML metadata shows that 1 link was created in the final HTML, which indicates the renderer successfully made the URL clickable. No fabricated or extraneous links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is maintained. The content flows logically from slide to slide, transitioning from the title to the data releases and finally the tasks. While converted to a single-column web layout, a user can easily find information in the same relative order as the source PDF. The placement of the checkmark icons at the bottom instead of within the list is a specific image handling issue addressed in V3 rather than a general layout failure.
There are no tables present in the source PDF or the HTML output.

In the source PDF on page 3, there are three green checkmark icons positioned next to specific sub-bullets under the 'Infrastructure buildout' section. These are meaningful icons indicating the completion status of tasks. In the HTML output, these icons are missing from the list items where they belong. Instead, a single green checkmark icon appears at the very bottom of the document, disconnected from its context. This makes the status information absent from the relevant content area.
checks.b_evidence_source: Three green checkmark icons next to bullet points 'Replicate data...', 'Replicate all map...', and 'Replicate geodatabases...' on page 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: A single green checkmark icon is rendered at the bottom of the page, after the 'Tasks' section, and is missing from the bulleted list.
checks.b_evidence_location: Tasks section, specifically sub-bullets under Infrastructure buildout (Page 3 of source).

checks.a_analysis: There are no instances of duplicated content blocks, paragraphs, or lists in the HTML output.

The overall visual quality is acceptable for an HTML conversion. The text is clear, spacing is consistent, and the structure is readable. Aside from the misplaced status icons (noted in V3), the document lacks excessive whitespace or formatting inconsistencies.
